headline: Sugar Tax Revenue Hits RM1.8B But Diabetes Rates Unchanged
context: The excise duty on sugary drinks introduced in 2019 collected RM1.8 billion cumulatively by 2025, but Malaysia's diabetes prevalence remained at 18.3% of adults, the highest in ASEAN.

cards.big: RM1.8 billion collected from sugar tax — yet 3.9 million Malaysians still have diabetes, up from 3.6 million
cards.sub: 40 sen per litre levy reduced consumption by only 7% while manufacturers reformulated to just below threshold

cards.big: Sugar tax revenue goes to consolidated fund — zero ringgit earmarked for diabetes prevention or treatment
cards.sub: MOH diabetes treatment costs RM4.8 billion annually — 2.7x the total sugar tax revenue collected since 2019

cards.big: B40 communities show 22.1% diabetes prevalence versus 14.2% among T20 — affordability drives poor diet
cards.sub: Cheapest calories in Malaysian shops are sugar-rich: RM1 buys 800 kcal of biscuits but only 200 kcal of fruit

cards.big: Health Ministry has no authority to mandate sugar content reduction — only Customs enforces excise thresholds
cards.sub: Inter-ministry coordination failure means tax policy and health policy operate in separate silos

cards.big: A sugar tax that raises revenue without reducing sugar consumption is just another consumption tax with a healthy label
Should 100% of sugar tax revenue be ring-fenced for diabetes prevention programmes in B40 communities?
